what_it_does: Lists six JSX canvas files in the claude-ds project as a batch allocation for an interpretive build wave, recording each file's project, relative path, and filesystem root.
observations: Contains six entries, all for project 'claude-ds' and root '/home/tim/company/design/claude-ds'
All paths are under 'app/canvases/' and end in '.jsx'
Canvas names: Inbox, Overview, Patterns, Registry, RegistryInspector, Settings
No code symbols or dependencies are included
File is named batch-005.json under wave-002/alloc
standouts: All entries are JSX canvas components
Includes both Registry.jsx and RegistryInspector.jsx as a related pair
No extracted symbols despite being in an interpretive layer
concerns: Batch grouping rationale is not stated
No version, timestamp, or ordering significance provided
Absence of symbols limits interpretive value
notes: A manifest of six canvas JSX files in claude-ds, useful as a scoping list but carrying no internal code detail.
questions: Why are these six canvases grouped in batch-005?
Does the order of entries imply processing sequence?
Is the lack of symbols intentional or an extraction gap?
intention_signals: It appears to enumerate a set of UI canvas views selected for interpretive processing or build orchestration within a larger ledger of code/design substrates.
contribution: Provides an addressable, scoped list of canvas entry points for the claude-ds project that a fused system can use to locate and target further analysis or generation.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON batch manifest listing six JSX canvas files in the claude-ds project under app/canvases, with project name and filesystem root for each entry.
intention_for_embedding: To record a selected group of canvas components for interpretive processing in wave-002 batch-005.
